# Transcrição: M2-launchstory

- **Fonte:** M2-launchstory
- **Duração:** 08:09
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

## [00:00 - 03:02]

I can remember getting this email like it was yesterday. I mean it was just so vivid in my mind. I'd never seen any email like this before. It was completely different. This was a long time ago. In fact it was so different that I actually had to... I printed it out. This was back in the days of the the dot matrix printer before I could afford a laser printer or they didn't have inkjet printer. So anyways I printed this thing out and it was page after page. I don't know. Must have been six, seven, eight pages long. This email and the reason it was so different, it was a sales letter. It was a sales letter for a product that was called Turn Your Computer Into A Money Machine. Now at that point I had no business. I had never even heard of this information business, this information marketing business, this idea of selling your knowledge, selling... creating information or knowledge products and selling them. Something I'd never even heard of. So I got this advertiser for Turn Your Computer Into A Money Machine. I know that sounds really cheesy right now but it's what it was. And I printed it out and I sat there and I read this thing over and over and over and it was advertising this course that cost $97 plus shipping and handling. So it was $99.50. I had very very little money at the time. The disposable income for my family for the entire year. Meaning the disposable... The money we had after we paid for our food and our rent and our insurance was $400 a year. So to spend $99.50 on this seemed ludicrous. But it just gave me this vision for this bigger life, for starting a business. It gave me this vision for having some control in my life, for being able to support my family. At that point I was Mr. Mom. I was staying home taking care of two kids, two young kids. My daughter was just a baby then. My son was just a few years old. My wife was out supporting the family and all I wanted to do was be able to help support the family. So I got this product or I got this advertisement, this email. I was advertising this product and I read it over and over and over again. For a week I just kept on going back to it. It just seemed to make so much sense but it seemed like such a leap that I could actually sell my knowledge. That anyone would buy anything from me. Just seemed like this unbelievable leap. But then I made this decision that changed everything. And I said, boy you know what? If I look around this publishing industry, I see other people publishing and making money. I know the Wall Street Journal has been published for a hundred years and they're still publishing it. They must be making money. So I know people make money through publishing. Maybe I can. So I wrote out a check and I sent the check off to buy that product. Turn your computer into a money machine. I got that back and that taught me the basics of information marketing. And because of that product, a few years later I was producing my own products.

## [03:02 - 06:02]

And then here we are, I don't know, 15, 16, 17 years later and I've now sold over 20 million dollars of products. So the reason that I tell you that story is not so much for the point of the story. I just wanted to illustrate how a story can pull you in. Did that one get you interested at all? Did it... Were you sort of wondering what was coming next? Why I was telling you the story? Stories are incredibly powerful. They are unbelievable... Unbelievably powerful. And they will set you apart from your competitors. They will set you apart from your market. And the beautiful thing about product launches is they allow you to... They give you the time and the space to tell a story. And you want to turn your marketing into a story. You want to always be delivering your content and your marketing as a story. Really what we want to do is create the story that starts at the beginning of your launch and arcs through your entire launch and really grabs people's attention. This is how you cut through the fog of marketing and really grab people. When you use stories, you pull people into your marketing. That's why you want to use stories. You want to grab their attention. You want to create anticipation. And you want them to just hanging on wanting to know what's coming next in the story. They're wondering how does this thing turn out? That's what keeps people reading a book. That's what keeps people... Hollywood movies two hours long. How do you keep people engaged for two hours by telling a story? Stories are how... They're how lessons have been passed down forever. Since the beginning of time. Since before we even had written language. Back then we had stories and those stories were how wisdom was passed from one generation to another generation to another generation. That's what makes them so powerful. We are bred. It's right in our DNA to pay attention to stories, to learn from stories. Once we start to hear a story, we just all the filters go off and we believe that story. Stories are really sticky. Meaning that people really remember them. People remember stories a lot longer than they're going to remember any lecture or any lesson. I mean if you think back to like a seminar or a workshop or a class that you've been to, just think back to something that maybe a class that you were in or seminar you were in two or three, four years ago or ten years ago. Try to remember something from that seminar, that workshop, that class. And I bet you that if you think about it, the odds are that you're going to remember a story. And if you actually remember a lesson or any real content, it's probably because it was shared as part of a story. It was wrapped into a story. Stories are what we remember. And the other key thing about stories is that we believe stories. They drop our defenses and it's like we're back in

## [06:02 - 08:09]

kindergarten again and we're just sitting there listening to the teacher. We believe stories. Stories are how we learn. Now obviously you have to be careful. You don't want to abuse this and you want to be truthful in your marketing and you want to be truthful in your stories. But the thing is, is people will believe your stories way more than if you just start telling them, you know, the benefits or the features of your product. If this has got six DVDs or this drill can turn it 20,000 RPM. They're not going to remember that. They're going to remember your stories. Now I'm a bit of a geek about stories. I've studied them quite a bit. And if you're interested in this topic, the place I think you should start with is Joseph Campbell and his hero's journey. It's an amazing body of work. It's a body of work for its entire lifetime in it and it really boils down the essence of storytelling throughout time and throughout the world. But the important thing to remember is that just about any story, just any story, is about, it's about the protagonist. And it's about that, you know, whether, and this is a matter if it's a comedy, if it's a tragedy, if it's a romantic story, it's all about a protagonist, the hero's efforts to secure some type of, you know, whatever they desire. Generally, they're fighting for this thing, usually at great cost, something that they desire. And the protagonist, generally they have to flirt with disaster before they can achieve their aims and get what they're after and live happily ever after. That's the master core formula behind the story. You've got a hero and they're, or an anti-hero, you've got a protagonist who is fighting the odds, fighting disaster to try to get what they're going after. It's about their struggles to overcome whatever, you know, whatever's on that path to what they're looking for. So what does all this have to do with your marketing? What does it have to do with your product launches? And what does it have to do with your business? That's what the next video is all about.
